title: oxalis stricta
intro: Oxalis stricta, also known as wood sorrel, is a perennial plant that belongs to the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). It is native to North America and has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its high concentration of oxalic acid, which has been traditionally used to treat urinary tract infections and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, making it beneficial for reducing inflammation and improving overall health, including lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Oxalis stricta can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and it is also used in some herbal remedies to treat digestive issues and boost the immune system.
